ogTitle: Lower Wilmot vs Loyetea: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lower Wilmot and Loyetea.
heading: Lower Wilmot vs Loyetea — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lower Wilmot, Tasmania and Loyetea, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Loyetea has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Lower Wilmot (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lower Wilmot or Loyetea safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lower Wilmot has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Loyetea scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lower Wilmot scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Loyetea scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lower Wilmot or Loyetea?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Loyetea has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
